senses.definition: plural of septic, referring to people who are sick or to medical septic conditions; in some contexts, outdated or humorous usage referring to septic tanks or septic systems.
examples: The town council discussed the concerns of the septics living near the old plant.
In old medical texts, septics were associated with infections.

senses.definition: a period of seven days; a week (archaic or poetic use in some contexts).
examples: He took a septier to rest after the voyage.
The manuscript speaks of a septier of festivities.

senses.definition: having seven varieties or kinds; sevenfold in some sense.
examples: The septifarious collection covered seven distinct themes.
A septifarious approach was adopted to the problem.

senses.definition: bearing seven or containing seven units; seven-bearing.
examples: The septiferous ore contained seven distinct minerals.
A septiferous leaf showed seven markings.

senses.definition: producing seven streams or tones; not a common established word outside playful coinage.
examples: The septifluous bells rang, each note distinct.
A septifluous voice carried seven overtones.

senses.definition: having seven leaves or seven folia; septimially foliate.
examples: The plant is septifolious, with seven leaflets.
A septifolious pattern appeared in the herbarium.

senses.definition: having seven forms or shapes; seven-part morphology.
examples: The septiform crystal displayed seven faces.
A septiform cluster was observed under the microscope.

senses.definition: breaking into seven parts; dividing into seven fragments.
examples: The septifragal vessel split into seven segments.
A septifragal analysis revealed seven subcomponents.

senses.definition: in a septifragal (seven-part) manner.
examples: The structure was septifragally organized.
The system operates septifragally across modules.

senses.definition: having seven sides.
examples: A septilateral polygon was drawn for the geometry problem.
The septilateral shape stood out on the graph.

senses.definition: an angle of 51.428571 degrees; in astrology, a 1/7 aspect.
examples: In astrology, the planet formed a septile with the moon.
The septile aspect is relatively rare in natal charts.

senses.definition: a number equal to 10^24 in the short scale (American) or 10^42 in the long scale (historical); a very large quantity.
examples: There are septillions of stars in the observable universe (figurative).
The contract involved a septillion dollars in theoretical value.
synonyms: vigintillion (short scale for 10^63) in some contexts; note this is not exact

senses.definition: plural form of septillion; very large numbers in counting.
examples: Models sometimes simulate trillions or septillions of particles.
Historical texts used septillions when describing vast quantities.

senses.definition: one septillionth part of something; 1/10^24 (short scale) or 1/10^42 (long scale).
examples: A septillionth of a meter is far smaller than a nanometer.
They measured with precision down to a septillionth.

senses.definition: plural of septillionth; very small fractions.
examples: Sensors needed accuracy to septillionths of a meter.
The data was accurate to septillionths.

senses.definition: relating to the number seven; based on seven in a system of numbers.
examples: The septimal scale uses seven notes per octave.
Septimal ratios appear in some tuning systems.

senses.definition: a week; a seven-day period (Latin, used in English in some scholarly contexts).
examples: The festival lasted for a septimana.
Reports were compiled over a septimana.

senses.definition: plural of septimana; weeks in Latin usage.
examples: Two septimanæ passed before the next council meeting.
Old chronicles record multiple septimanae of campaigns.

senses.definition: relating to a seven-day period; weekly.
examples: Septimanal reports are issued every Monday.
The septimanal cycle governs the calendar in some systems.

senses.definition: a person who studies or is interested in septimanals; a weekly-focused scholar or enthusiast (rare).
examples: The septimanarian kept records of weekly cycles.
A septimanarian would enjoy historical calendars.

senses.definition: the seventh musical note in the diatonic scale; also a unit of time in music, the seventh degree.
examples: She sang the note septime perfectly.
In the scale, la is followed by septime in some modes.

senses.definition: the seventh time; plural of septime in some contexts or a count of seventh occurrences.
examples: He tried septimes to get it right.
They met on several septimes to discuss progress.

senses.definition: inflammation of the septum and surrounding tissues (medical term, extremely rare).
examples: The patient suffered from septimetritis following surgery.
Septimetritis is a hypothetical or historical condition in some texts.

senses.definition: a unit of seven millimoles (hypothetical or rare), or a sevenfold mole context in specialized usage.
examples: The solution contained septimoles per liter.
Septimole concentration was noted in the report.

senses.definition: plural of septimole; seven-mole units in a solution or theoretical context.
examples: The solution contained several septimoles of solute.
Septimoles were calculated for the reaction.
